commonRoofingChallenges: Reputation-sensitive clientele expecting white-glove project management
Estate-scale projects with larger crews, longer timelines, and more complex logistics
Premium materials (slate, copper, tile, designer asphalt) standard rather than upgrade

uniqueAngleElaboration: Ladue is St. Louis's oldest-money market, and roofing here looks different from anywhere else in the metro. Estate-scale houses, premium materials as a baseline, strict municipal code enforcement, and reputation-sensitive clientele who notice every aspect of a project — from the tarp coverage on landscaping to the magnetic-sweep cleanup — make Ladue work a different category of contracting. The materials on the table are slate, copper, clay tile, designer asphalt at the high end of the line, and the workmanship has to match. Crews need to behave like guests rather than tradespeople: clean trucks, uniformed staff, scheduled communications with the homeowner or property manager, no profanity within earshot. None of that is optional. Code enforcement is among the strictest in the metro — the City of Ladue building department reviews permits carefully and inspects work thoroughly. A contractor who's never worked in Ladue before will fail this market quickly; one who has built a track record here will earn referrals from neighbors who already know the contractor's name.
